problem text: Your firm has a $20\%$ probability of being bought out between Monday night and Tuesday morning. On Monday, the price of a single share is $30$, however, if the acquisition occurs, there is a $50\%$ probability that a share will be valued at $50$ and a $50\%$ probability that a share will be valued at $x$. If the acquisition does not occur, there is a $60\%$ probability that a share will be valued at $0$ and a $40\%$ probability that a share will be valued at $10$. A fair agreement that grants the purchaser the right but not the obligation to acquire the stock at $40$ on Tuesday is worth $15$. Find $x$.
problem solution: We need to calculate the expected gain from acquiring the agreement. Specifically, if we obtain the agreement, we will only exercise our option to buy the stock at $40$ on Thursday if the stock's price surpasses $40$. Let $G$ signify the gain realized after acquiring the agreement.  $\mathbb{E}[G] = -15 + 0.2 \cdot 0.5 \cdot (50 - 40) + 0.2 \cdot 0.5 \cdot (x - 40)$  A fair valuation for the agreement implies $\mathbb{E}[G] = 0$.  $15 = 1 + 0.1x - 4$  $18 = 0.1x$  $x = 180.$
problem hint: We need to calculate the expected gain from acquiring the agreement. Specifically, if we obtain the agreement, we will only exercise our option to buy the stock at $40$ on Thursday if the stock's price surpasses $40$.
